title: ESA to seek lunar mapping capability for Argonaut lander
summary: ESA will seek external lunar topographic data for the design and potential first mission of its Argonaut lunar lander. The agency aims to leverage existing lunar mapping capabilities.

title: Beck: Iridium acquisition the “logical next step” for Rocket Lab
summary: Rocket Lab's CEO Peter Beck describes the acquisition of Iridium as a strategic move to expand into the space services market. The deal is seen as a logical progression for the company's growth ambitions.

title: Discovering the Universe – Astrophysics Flagship Space Observatories Offer Insights to Key Questions
summary: BAE Systems is highlighted as a key partner in NASA's astrophysics flagship space observatories, enabling technology to study distant galaxies, exoplanets, and their atmospheres. The article emphasizes BAE Systems' role in advancing astrophysics research and expanding our understanding of the universe.

title: Quantum computing is about to become a national security problem in orbit
summary: Quantum computing's rapid advancement threatens to break modern encryption, posing a national security risk in space. This development has significant implications for the commercial space industry.

title: Rocket Lab to acquire Iridium
summary: Rocket Lab is acquiring satellite telecommunications company Iridium for $8 billion to expand its space industry offerings. This move aims to position Rocket Lab as an end-to-end space company.

title: SatVu restarts commercial operations with HotSat-2
summary: SatVu has resumed commercial operations with its HotSat-2 satellite, following the failure of its debut satellite in low Earth orbit in 2023. The British thermal imaging startup now offers revenue-generating services.

title: Craft Prospect selected for The Sunday Times Scotland Fast 50
summary: Glasgow-based space engineering company Craft Prospect has been included in The Sunday Times Scotland Fast 50, a new annual list highlighting fast-growing companies. This recognition underscores Craft Prospect's growth and contributions to the space industry.

title: China establishes VLEO industry alliance as satellites demonstrate sustained low-orbit operations
summary: China has formed a national industry alliance focused on very low Earth orbit (VLEO) operations, as satellites demonstrate sustained activities below 300 kilometers and propulsion startups gain investment.

title: Rocket Lab launches 10th Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its 10th radar-imaging satellite for Japanese company Synspective on June 26, following a delay due to a responsive space mission.

title: Spain-backed fund joins FOSSA’s sovereign satellite communications push
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems raised $10.5 million for its sovereign satellite communications constellation, with funding from a Spain-backed technology investment vehicle. The round aims to expand FOSSA's connectivity services.

title: Light pollution may be erasing millions of dollars in value at US dark-sky parks
summary: Light pollution is significantly reducing the economic value of US dark-sky parks by affecting visitor experiences. Researchers used satellite data and surveys to quantify this financial impact.

title: Mars orbiter watches Perseverance rover cross the marathon finish line | Space photo of the day for June 29, 2026
summary: NASA's Mars orbiter captured an image of the Perseverance rover completing a marathon on Mars. The rover continues to explore the Red Planet, raising hopes for future long-distance exploration.

title: How NASA's 'America 250' celebrations are reaching from the sky to the moon
summary: NASA is celebrating America's 250th anniversary with events involving aircraft and spacecraft. The celebrations will take place around July 4, marking the anniversary of the Declaration of Independence's ratification.

title: The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered a massive, densely packed galaxy cluster from 'cosmic noon,' defying prior scientific expectations. Scientists are puzzled by the unexpected structure, which challenges current models of early galaxy formation.

title: The Strawberry Moon rises tonight. Here's what to expect from June's full moon
summary: The Strawberry Moon, June's first full moon of summer, will rise tonight (June 29) with a golden glow and an optical illusion. This celestial event marks the start of summer's full moons.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites into Earth orbit on Falcon 9 launch from California (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites into Earth orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on June 28, 2026.

title: Japanese probe set for super-close flyby on July 5: 'We're going to discover another beast to put in the zoo of asteroids'
summary: The Japanese Hayabusa2 probe is set for a super-close flyby of an asteroid on July 5, marking one of the closest and riskiest space-rock flybys in history. After delivering samples from the asteroid Ryugu to Earth, the mission aims to explore another celestial body.

title: Watch China's Tiangong space station cross the moon in stunning amateur footage
summary: Amateur footage captures China's Tiangong space station crossing in front of the moon near Tycho Crater. The video showcases the station's transit against the lunar surface.

title: SpaceX launches 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite to orbit from Florida
summary: SpaceX successfully launched a 15,000-pound SiriusXM satellite into orbit from Florida's Space Coast on June 28. This marks another significant mission for SpaceX in deploying commercial satellites.

title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Preps for Spacewalk
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing for a spacewalk by testing his spacesuit aboard the International Space Station with assistance from ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot. The spacewalk is scheduled for June 2026.

title: Mapping Earth’s Observations, featuring Betsy Ford
summary: NASA's Earth-observing satellites track various environmental phenomena, providing critical data for science, policy, agriculture, and climate research. The Earth Science Division (ESD) manages this extensive portfolio.

title: NextSTEP-3 A: Lunar Enabling Technology
summary: NASA issued a draft Broad Agency Announcement under NextSTEP-3, Appendix A, to advance lunar and cislunar technology readiness. The solicitation targets vertical solar arrays, ISRU oxygen production, and Stirling radioisotope generators.

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input to Accelerate Lunar Surface Technologies
summary: NASA is collaborating with industry to speed up the development of lunar surface technologies by seeking input on a draft solicitation. The goal is to create cohesive systems for long-term lunar exploration and reduce risks through joint design, development, testing, and evaluation.

title: NASA Announces Winners for 2026 Human Lander Challenge
summary: NASA revealed the winners of the 2026 Human Lander Challenge, which focused on student-developed environmental control and life support systems for future lunar landers. The competition supports the Artemis program aimed at returning astronauts to the Moon.

title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
summary: NASA is testing a new refueling device for in-space missions, enabling spacecraft to refuel in Earth orbit before deep space exploration. This technology could be crucial for future missions requiring orbital refueling.

title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
summary: A mission to raise the orbit of NASA's Swift observatory is scheduled for launch on June 30. Partners and NASA are preparing for the Swift Boost Mission.

title: NASA Identifies More Than 40 Space Technologies for Collaboration
summary: NASA selected 41 proposals from 37 companies to advance space technologies supporting long-term lunar presence and Mars exploration. The selected American companies will develop solutions for space transportation, planetary surface operations, and lunar missions.

title: Euclid Sees Heart of Milky Way
summary: Euclid, an ESA mission with NASA contributions, captured a new image of the heart of the Milky Way galaxy. The observation aligns with a region that NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will study later this summer.

title: Bringing Signals to NASA
summary: Eric Fernandez shares his childhood fascination with rocket launches and his eventual career in the space industry, inspired by his grandparents' service at Vandenberg Air Force Base.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help study stellar populations and galactic structure.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate visitors about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's spaceflight schedule features six orbital launches globally, including the inaugural flight of Pegasus XL. The launches will occur alongside missions by SpaceX's Falcon 9 and Chinese launch vehicles.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle on Tuesday morning. The test was launched aboard a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 after the CRS-34 mission departs. The busy month for the crew includes maintenance and operational tasks.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space is advancing its Terran R heavy-lift rocket, with its second stage now transported to Stennis Space Center. The company is on track for a 2026 debut of the vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is headquartered in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks prior.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It suggests this innovation could significantly impact future space missions and operations.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science by involving research institutes in missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and select materials to ensure spacecraft survive the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by addressing materials science challenges.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: ISS Risk Margin ‘Alarming’ As NASA Nears 2030 Extension Date
summary: NASA's advisory committee warns that the risk margin for regular International Space Station (ISS) operations has reached an alarming level as the agency approaches the 2030 extension date. The ISS is facing increasing challenges in maintaining safe and sustainable operations.

title: SpaceX Alum Have Raised $9.2B, New Data Shows
summary: SpaceX alumni have collectively raised $9.2 billion in funding for their new ventures, highlighting the company's role as a talent incubator. The data underscores SpaceX's impact on fostering innovation and entrepreneurship in the space industry.

title: Firefly Acquires Autonomous Space Ops Company
summary: Firefly Aerospace acquired Space-ng, an AI-powered autonomous guidance systems provider. The acquisition aims to enhance Firefly's space operations capabilities.

title: Europe Expands its Launch Opportunities to New Companies
summary: Europe is increasing launch opportunities for new companies to meet growing demand. This expansion aims to support emerging players in the space industry.

title: Exclusive: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic Partner on Security Demo Mission
summary: SpaceComputer and Spacemanic have partnered to conduct a security demonstration mission focused on addressing hacking threats in orbit and shared-compute modules on rideshare satellites. Space Fabric, SpaceComputer's solution, aims to enhance on-orbit security amid growing cyber risks.

title: Japan Eyes Sovereign D2D Satellite Network
summary: Japan plans to select a proposal for its domestically owned and operated D2D satellite network, called J-LEO, this month. The initiative aims to enhance sovereign satellite communication capabilities.

title: Satellite Operators Launch Trade Association
summary: Amazon, Globalstar, Iridium, and Canada’s Telesat have formed the SpaceConnect Association, a new trade group for non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite operators. This collaboration aims to advocate for the interests of NGSO operators in the evolving space industry.

title: SpaceX Starfall Demo Provides Validation for Reentry Startups
summary: SpaceX launched a demo mission of its Starfall reentry capsule, marking a significant step in the reentry sector. The event signals growing competition and validation for emerging reentry startups.

title: Commercial Markets Are Ready for EO — But the Understanding Gap Persists
summary: The EO Summit in London highlighted the readiness of commercial markets for Earth Observation (EO) solutions, but noted a persistent gap in understanding how to effectively communicate these solutions to customers.

title: Calian to Acquire Canadian Service Provider Galaxy Broadband
summary: Calian Group is set to acquire Galaxy Broadband Communications for up to CAD $52 million ($36.6 million) to expand its satellite communications and remote connectivity solutions. The deal aims to strengthen Calian's position in the Canadian satellite services market.

title: Tototheo Global to Deploy SES FlexMaritime in Expanded Maritime Deal
summary: Tototheo Global has expanded its agreement with SES to deploy the FlexMaritime connectivity service for the maritime market. The service combines SES's satellite network with integrated solutions.

title: ReOrbit Secures Finnish Funding for ‘OrbitCloud’ R&D Program
summary: ReOrbit has secured 4.6 million euro ($5.3 million) in Finnish funding for its OrbitCloud R&D program, which aims to develop a space-based edge computing network.

title: AiRanaculus to Advance Networking Technologies for NASA Lunar and Space Communications Networks
summary: AiRanaculus secured a $5 million NASA contract to develop networking technologies for lunar and space communications networks. The award aims to enhance NASA's infrastructure for future space missions.

title: Infleqtion Teams With Voyager in New Quantum Space Initiative
summary: Infleqtion, Voyager Technologies, and other partners launched a new quantum space initiative to advance U.S. quantum space capabilities. The collaboration includes Monarch Quantum, Armada, and the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Pegasus Launch Set to Deploy Servicer Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus rocket is scheduled to launch a servicing spacecraft to reboost NASA's Swift Observatory before it re-enters Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to extend the observatory's operational lifespan.

title: Mitsubishi Electric Contracts MDA Space for Digital Payload for Japanese Defense Satellite
summary: MDA Space has secured a contract with Mitsubishi Electric Corporation to build a digital payload, antennas, and other components for a next-generation Japanese defense communications satellite. This collaboration highlights MDA Space's role in advancing Japan's defense satellite capabilities.

title: Capella Validates Mynaric Optical Terminal on Latest SAR Satellite
summary: Capella Space validated a Mynaric optical communications terminal on its latest SAR satellite, marking the first deployment of an optical terminal by Capella Space. The company also released the first synthetic aperture radar images from the satellite.

title: BAE Systems to Build Buses for Vantor’s Vantage Imaging Satellites
summary: BAE Systems will construct satellite buses for Vantor's Vantage imaging satellites, with the first two satellites expected to be operational by 2029.
